results.code:
351 
352 def verify_webhook_secret(
353     secret_header: str | None, expected: str, admin_secret: str = ""
354 ) -> None:
355     """Verify the webhook secret header against expected secret or ADMIN_SECRET.
356 
357     Allows authentication via either:
358     1. WEBHOOK_SECRET (dedicated webhook secret)
359     2. ADMIN_SECRET (environment admin secret)
360 
361     In development mode (when both secrets are empty), webhooks are allowed without auth.
362 
363     Args:
364         secret_header: The secret provided in the request header
365         expected: The expected WEBHOOK_SECRET value
366         admin_secret: Optional ADMIN_SECRET value to also accept
367 
368     Raises:
369         HTTPException: 401 if secret is invalid, None if allowed
370     """
371     # Development mode: allow webhooks without secret if no secrets configured
372     if not expected and not admin_secret:
373         logger.warning(
374             "[WEBHOOK] No webhook secret configured - allowing webhook request (dev mode)"
375         )
376         return
377 
378     if not secret_header:
379         raise HTTPException(
380             status_code=HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Missing webhook secret"
381         )
382 
383     # Use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks
384     from secrets import compare_digest
385 
386     # Accept either webhook_secret or ADMIN_SECRET
387     expected_match = compare_digest(secret_header, expected) if expected else False
388     admin_match = compare_digest(secret_header, admin_secret) if admin_secret else False
389 
390     if not (expected_match or admin_match):
391         logger.warning("[WEBHOOK] Invalid webhook secret attempt")
392         raise HTTPException(
393             status_code=HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Invalid webhook secret"
394         )
395
